index_subtitle: Explore different ways to transmit data
wired_desc: Copper lines (twisted pair) and optical fibers for data transmission over hundreds of kilometers.
wireless_desc: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, 5G, satellite communications — freedom of movement without cables.
interfaces_desc: USB, HDMI, Thunderbolt — cables and connectors for devices.

index_subtitle: Explore different ways to transmit data
wired_desc: Copper lines (twisted pair) and optical fibers for data transmission over hundreds of kilometers.
wireless_desc: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, 5G, satellite communications — freedom of movement without cables.
interfaces_desc: USB, HDMI, Thunderbolt — cables and connectors for devices.
footer_text: © 2025-2026 Data Transmission: From Copper to Fiber. Educational Project.
usb_desc: Connector types: USB-A, USB-B, USB-C. Versions: 2.0 (480 Mbit/s), 3.2 (20 Gbit/s), USB4 (40 Gbit/s). USB-C supports data, video and power up to 240W (USB PD).
hdmi_desc: Video and audio transmission. Version 2.1 supports 8K@60Hz, 4K@120Hz, dynamic HDR metadata, eARC.
displayport_desc: Alternative to HDMI, often used in monitors. Version 2.0 delivers up to 77.4 Gbit/s, supports 16K.
thunderbolt_desc: USB-C based interface providing up to 40 Gbit/s, PCIe and display support, daisy-chaining capability.
vga_desc: Legacy analog (VGA) and digital (DVI) video interfaces, found in older equipment.
